classification.authority_posture: intent-preserving derived control surface; not doctrine; not earned truth
classification.scope: current repo-held runtime and A2 memory surfaces

maker_intent.summary: All my intent, and the stuff I am writing, are first-class things to be saved and in the very graphs. My intentions. The intent is a first-class thing to refine. And extracting the system and its maker's intent is the most important thing to be refined and preserved. The whole point of the persistent memory and A2 and A1 brains: not losing context, and saving that context continuously as the system runs. | Skills and graphs should be fully integrated and mature. The system should self-heal, self-improve, and self-optimize as a live closed-loop controller, not just a skill library. | The system derives skills from: Lev nonclassical runtime design doc, Z3 constraint solver, Karpathy's design philosophy, lev-os/agents, lev-os/leviathan/workshop, and JP's skill ideas. These are not just references — they are first-class sources to be continuously refined into operational skills.

constraints.label: intent_first_class

constraints.label: context_continuity
constraints.statement: Continuously preserve runtime context so active intent and reasoning state are not lost.

constraints.label: skill_graph_co_development
constraints.statement: Develop skills and graphs together, with execution tested against refinery-backed graph surfaces.
constraints.rationale: Derived from maker intent statements about joint graph/skill maturation.

constraints.label: graph_native_memory
constraints.statement: Persist control-relevant intent and context in graph-native and append-only memory surfaces.
constraints.rationale: Derived from maker intent statements about graph storage and persistent memory.
